topic: Area of Study 4: Popular Music 1980 to Present
subject: music

items.q: Is sampling the same as covering a song?
items.correct: No - a sample reuses the actual original recording, while a cover is a new performance of the song by different musicians.
items.misconception: Sampling just means playing someone else's song yourself.
items.random_wrong: Sampling means giving away free copies of a single.
items.why: The legal and musical issues differ completely: samples need clearance of the recording itself, and producers manipulate the borrowed audio rather than re-performing it.

items.q: Is Auto-Tune only used to rescue bad singers?
items.correct: No - besides subtle correction, artists deliberately exaggerate it as a creative robotic vocal effect.
items.misconception: Hearing Auto-Tune proves the singer cannot sing.
items.random_wrong: Auto-Tune is a machine that writes the song's melody automatically.
items.why: Since the late 1990s the hard Auto-Tune sound has been an intentional stylistic choice in pop and hip-hop, not just a hidden fix.

items.q: Is a remix just the original song made louder?
items.correct: No - a remix reworks the recorded elements, often changing the beat, structure, instrumentation and length for a new context.
items.misconception: Remixing means re-releasing the same track with boosted volume.
items.random_wrong: A remix is the band's first rehearsal recording.
items.why: Remix culture treats the multitrack stems as raw material; understanding this explains how one song exists in radio, club and extended versions.

items.q: Why was the launch of MTV significant for pop music?
items.correct: It made the music video a central promotional tool
items.why: The answer examiners want is: It made the music video a central promotional tool. “It banned electronic instruments” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.correct: Reusing part of an existing recording in a new track
items.random_wrong: Testing a speaker before a gig
items.why: Correct answer: Reusing part of an existing recording in a new track. “Auditioning singers for a band” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Classic 1980s synth-pop is primarily built from which sound sources?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Synthesisers and drum machines. “String quartet and harpsichord” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Auto-Tune is best described as what?
items.correct: Software that corrects or stylises vocal pitch
items.misconception: A method of tuning drum kits
items.why: It's Software that corrects or stylises vocal pitch. “A method of tuning drum kits” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year did the music television channel MTV launch?
items.why: Exact answer: 1981. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
